claimText: The Global Geoparks Network (GGN) is a non-profit International Association founded under UNESCO that establishes ethical standards for Global Geoparks.
evidenceExcerpt: GGN is a non-profit International Association founded under UNESCO, which establishes ethical standards to be adopted by Global Geoparks.
examUse: Statement-based question on the nature and parent body of GGN.
distractorHooks: GGN is under UNEP or IUCN instead of UNESCO

claimText: UNESCO Global Geoparks (UGGP) were introduced in 2015 as a component of the International Geosciences and Geoparks Programme (IGGP).
evidenceExcerpt: Introduced in 2015 as a key component of the International Geosciences and Geoparks Programme (IGGP).
examUse: Statement-based question on the origin and parent programme of UGGP.
distractorHooks: Introduced under the Man and the Biosphere (MAB) Programme
Introduced in 1972 along with World Heritage Sites

claimText: The UNESCO Global Geopark (UGGP) status is not permanent and is subject to reassessment every four years.
evidenceExcerpt: UGGP status is not permanent; it is reassessed every four years.
examUse: Statement-based question on the permanence of UGGP status.

claimText: Membership in the Global Geoparks Network (GGN) is mandatory for all UNESCO Global Geoparks.
evidenceExcerpt: Mandatory Networking: Membership of the GGN is obligatory for UGGPs.
examUse: Statement-based question on the relationship between UGGP and GGN.
distractorHooks: Membership in GGN is voluntary for UGGPs
Only developing countries must join GGN

claimText: Currently, there are no UNESCO Global Geoparks located in India.
evidenceExcerpt: Current Status: 229 UNESCO Global Geoparks in 50 countries (none in India).
examUse: Statement-based question on India's representation in the UGGP list.
distractorHooks: India has multiple UNESCO Global Geoparks
The first UGGP in India was recently established
